task: Generate a novel, plausible, unifying hypothesis for: Why does biological aging occur?

final_hypothesis.name: Reference-Decay Hypothesis
final_hypothesis.one_line: Aging is the accumulation of confidently-defended calibration error in self-referential homeostatic control loops: feedback can only null the gap between state and an internally stored reference, and has no sensor for the true physiological optimum, so drift of the reference is an unobservable common-mode error that is defended rather than corrected.
final_hypothesis.novel_core: Aging as a control-OBSERVABILITY failure (reference drift lives in the loop's unobservable subspace), which unifies damage, hyperfunction, information-loss, resilience-loss, and deleteriome theories as facets.
final_hypothesis.intervention_note: Over-reset lifespan decline is emergent from absorbing identity-loss mortality; peak at partial reset, below-untreated at full erasure (well-differentiated cells).

workflow.action: Framed the bar: must UNIFY existing theories, be mechanistically precise/modelable, and be genuinely novel (not a rename).
Surveyed the aging-theory landscape via 12 targeted web searches, verifying identifiers.

Identified the unfilled gap: every strong theory treats set-point/reference DRIFT as the thing to explain, but none states WHY an error-correcting system fails to correct it. Control-theory answer: a loop cannot regulate what it cannot observe; the true optimum is unobservable.
Formulated the Reference-Decay hypothesis and its unification table (Table 1).
Built a minimal generative model encoding ONLY the mechanism; tested whether known laws of aging emerge unforced.

model.state: somatic unit reference r defended by fast feedback so state x~=r; fitness depends on true optimum x*=0 (WLOG); loop measures only e=x-r
model.soma_dynamics: dr = b*dt + s*dW  (biased random walk; b=directional hyperfunction bias, s=stochastic turnover noise)
model.mortality_model: unit fails if |r|>theta; hazard h=h0*exp(alpha*failed_fraction); death by hazard sampling. NO explicit age term.

workflow.action: Verified four emergent (non-tuned-for) signatures of aging.
Note: initial hazard params under-sampled deaths (~4% dead, weak fit); swept theta/alpha/h0 to a realistic regime (median~105, 96% mortality) before trusting Gompertz fit.

Tested the decisive differentiating prediction: rejuvenation as PARTIAL reference-reset. Faithful model: reference = legitimate differentiated set-point (identity, sd sigma_id) + accumulated drift; a reset of depth lambda removes fraction lambda of BOTH. Identity loss is a real mortality driver and failure is ABSORBING (dedifferentiated/senescent cells don't spontaneously re-specify).

results.interpretation: Median lifespan peaks at partial reset (lambda*~0.4 well-diff) then DECLINES; full erasure (lambda=1) falls BELOW untreated (66 vs 97). Cliff steepens with differentiation strength. Mirrors transient (rejuvenating) vs sustained/complete (lethal, identity-loss) OSKM reprogramming.

workflow.action: Recovered from an out-of-memory kernel kill on the long-horizon (T=220) intervention run by switching to float32 and a streaming failed-fraction computation (no full 3-D array copy per lambda).
Produced 3 publication-grade figures + 1 comparison table (figure-style skill), each QA'd with the render-then-verify bbox check.
Wrote result.md (~1,280 words + bottom-line box), reasoning.md, sources.md, and this trace into presentation/.

Post-audit corrections. (1) Rebuilt the intervention model so the over-reset lifespan decline is EMERGENT from absorbing identity-loss mortality, replacing an earlier version whose 'full erasure kills' claim was not supported by its own data (median plateaued at the ceiling). (2) Ran a Crossref API confirmation pass over all 14 DOIs (14/14 resolved; doi_verification.json), which resolves the corrected reference #3 DOI 10.1016/j.cell.2024.01.013 to the right title/journal/year and no longer contains the earlier wrong DOI. (3) Rewrote sources.md provenance to rest identifier correctness solely on the reproducible Crossref pass, and stopped claiming per-query web_search tool evidence I cannot enumerate. (4) Retracted an incorrect claim made to the auditor about how web_search renders in the transcript. (5) Fixed a stale 'lambda*~0.6' caption in this file's figures section to match the corrected lambda*~0.4.

figures.shows: Mechanism: soma controls to references it cannot re-calibrate; reference drift = unobservable subspace.
Four emergent laws of aging: Gompertz mortality (R2=0.94), rising cross-cell variance, critical slowing down (2.3x recovery time), soma-vs-germline divergence.

Rejuvenation = re-calibration: median lifespan peaks at partial reset (lambda*~0.4, well-differentiated cells) and DECLINES to below-untreated at full erasure (66 vs 97); cliff steepens with differentiation strength.
How Reference-Decay unifies 7 existing theory families.
Raw model arrays (references summary, hazard, variance, resilience) for reproducibility.

key_predictions_and_falsifiers: Old cells defend a SHIFTED set-point with high precision (not merely noisier around the correct one) — falsifier if the latter.
Re-referencing interventions (parabiosis, reprogramming) beat damage-repair-only interventions.
Reprogramming dose-response is non-monotonic with an identity-loss cliff.
Biological age = distance of epigenetic state from developmental optimum; reversible without genome repair.
Negligibly-senescent species show flat variance-vs-age and non-Gompertz mortality.
honest_limits: Conceptual/organizing hypothesis with a sufficiency (not fitted) toy model; 'reference/optimum/observability' need system-specific molecular identification; does not deny damage but reframes it as a driver of drift; x* is currently unobservable to us too — making it observable IS the proposed research program.
